# terrible_community_aws_dms_endpoint (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `endpointidentifier` (String) An identifier name for the endpoint.
- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `certificatearn` (String) Amazon Resource Name (ARN) for the certificate.
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `databasename` (String) Name for the database on the origin or target side.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `dmstransfersettings` (String) The settings in JSON format for the DMS transfer type of source endpoint.
- `dynamodbsettings` (String) Settings in JSON format for the target Amazon DynamoDB endpoint if source or target is dynamodb.
- `elasticsearchsettings` (String) Settings in JSON format for the target Elasticsearch endpoint.
- `endpointtype` (String) Type of endpoint we want to manage. Required when *state=present*.
- `enginename` (String) Database engine that we want to use, please refer to the AWS DMS for more information on the supported engines and their limitations. Required when *state=present*.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `externaltabledefinition` (String) The external table definition.
- `extraconnectionattributes` (String) Extra attributes for the database connection, the AWS documentation states " For more information about extra connection attributes, see the documentation section for your data store."
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `kinesissettings` (String) Settings in JSON format for the target Amazon Kinesis Data Streams endpoint.
- `kmskeyid` (String) Encryption key to use to encrypt replication storage and connection information.
- `mongodbsettings` (String) Settings in JSON format for the source MongoDB endpoint.
- `password` (String) Password used to connect to the database this attribute can only be written the AWS API does not return this parameter.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `port` (Number) TCP port for access to the database.
- `retries` (Number) number of times we should retry when deleting a resource Required when *wait=true*.
- `s3settings` (String) S3 buckets settings for the target Amazon S3 endpoint.
- `servername` (String) Servername that the endpoint will connect to.
- `serviceaccessrolearn` (String) Amazon Resource Name (ARN) for the service access role that you want to use to create the endpoint.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `sslmode` (String) Mode used for the SSL connection.
- `state` (String) State of the endpoint.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `username` (String) Username our endpoint will use to connect to the database.
- `wait` (Boolean) Whether Ansible should wait for the object to be deleted when *state=absent*.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `endpoint` (String) A description of the DMS endpoint.
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
